cards.back: Insertion Sort is a simple sorting algorithm that works by dividing the input into a sorted and an unsorted region. Each subsequent element from the unsorted region is inserted into the sorted region in its correct position.
The time complexity of Insertion Sort is O(n^2), where n is the number of elements in the list. However, it is relatively efficient for small data sets and can be useful for educational purposes.
The space complexity of Insertion Sort is O(1), as it only requires a single auxiliary array to store the temporary elements while the list is being sorted.

Insertion Sort works by iterating through the list one element at a time, inserting each element into its correct position in the sorted region. This process continues until the entire list has been sorted.

cards.front: What are the advantages of Insertion Sort?
cards.back: The advantages of Insertion Sort include its simplicity, stability, and adaptability to partially sorted lists. It is also relatively easy to implement and can be efficient for small data sets.
